title: Bold Modern Bookshelf (Custom Sizes)
summary: A configurable plywood carcass bookshelf with a chunky 2x4 face frame. You size it to your wall — so the cut list is generated, not fixed.
description:
A big, bold bookshelf with a substantial 2x4 face frame over a plywood (or 1x12) carcass — the kind of piece that anchors a wall. Because it's meant to be sized to your space, there's no single cut list: you generate the carcass dimensions from a configurator and the framing follows from the overall height and width. What's fixed is the method, and that's what this plan teaches.

The carcass is the skeleton. Keep any individual shelf span under about 36–40" or the shelves will bow under books over time — if your wall is wider, add a vertical divider (a bay) rather than stretching one shelf. Bay supports do double duty: they break up the span and they kill the side-to-side wobble a tall open carcass otherwise has. The front bottom bay support is required for the look; the top back one is optional structure.

The 2x4 face frame is the 'bold' part. If you have a table saw, rip the rounded factory edges off the 2x4s first so they finish at about 3-1/4" wide with crisp square edges — a rounded-over 2x4 reads as lumber, a square-edged one reads as furniture. Assemble the face frame with 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket screws, then trim the shelf fronts with 1x2 to hide the plywood edges.

tools.note: Carcass, bay supports, and 2x4 face frame all pocket-holed.
Rip the rounded edges off the 2x4 face frame — the square edge is the look.
For exposed plywood shelf edges if you skip the 1x2 trim anywhere.

materials.name: Plywood or 1x12 carcass boards
materials.note: Quantity/size from your configurator design; keep shelf spans under 36–40".

materials.name: 2x4 face frame boards
materials.note: Length needed: twice the overall height plus the overall width.

materials.name: 1x4 bay support boards
materials.note: Length needed: twice the overall width.

materials.name: 1x2 shelf trim
materials.note: Number of shelves (excluding top) x shelf length.

steps.title: Design the carcass
steps.body: Use a carcass configurator to design your carcass to your wall — the tool generates the cut list and materials for the plywood/1x12 box. Two rules override the tool: keep shelf spans under about 36–40" so shelves don't sag, and pick a total height that suits the use (30" for a console/media height, counter height, or full bookshelf height). Build the carcass box first.

steps.title: Add the bay supports
steps.body: Bay supports add structure to the carcass and stop the side-to-side wobble a tall open box has. The front bottom bay support is required for the look; the top back one is optional. Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the ends and along the top edges and install them.

steps.title: Build and attach the 2x4 face frame
steps.body: If you have a table saw, rip the rounded edges off the 2x4s first so they end up about 3-1/4" wide with square edges — this single step is what makes the frame read as furniture instead of lumber. Measure and cut the face-frame pieces to your carcass, join them with 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket screws, and attach the frame to the front of the carcass.

steps.title: Trim the shelves and finish
steps.body: Measure and cut 1x2 trim for every shelf front to cap the exposed plywood edges, and attach with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails. Fill nail holes, sand, and paint or stain. A painted finish suits the bold modern look; stain if you used a nice plywood face.

images.alt: Bold modern bookshelf with a chunky square-edged face frame and multiple bays
